title: Authorizing an opened window to access back to the originating window is security-sensitive
description: Authorizing an opened window to access back to the originating window is security-sensitiveAnalyze your codeintentionality-completesecuritySecurity HotspotcwephishingA newly opened window having access back to the originating window could allow basic phishing attacks (thewindow.openerobject is notnulland thuswindow.opener.locationcan be set to a malicious website by the opened page).For instance, an attacker can put a link (say: "http://example.com/mylink") on a popular website that changes, when opened, the original page to "http://example.com/fake_login". On "http://example.com/fake_login" there is a fake login page which could trick real users to enter their credentials.Ask Yourself WhetherThe application opens untrusted external URL.There is a risk if you answered yes to this question.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant: window.open('http://example.com'); // Allows opened window to access opener

checks.message: Use 'noopener' parameter with window.open() to prevent security risks.
